hero.name: Amir H. Golnabi, Ph.D.
hero.title: MedTech R&D Leader | Scientific & People Leadership
hero.pitch:
I am a MedTech R&D leader with 15+ years building high-performing teams and translating biomedical science, computational modeling, and data-driven innovation into clinically impactful therapies and products. As Sr. Staff Scientist at Fresenius Medical Care, I lead peritoneal dialysis R&D from early-stage research through translational product development in a regulated healthcare environment — driving scientific strategy, laboratory operations, IP development, and cross-functional execution across engineering, clinical, regulatory, and product teams. 
I lead by enabling people. I build and mentor multidisciplinary teams, create environments where technical rigor and innovation thrive, and consistently deliver results under resource-constrained, ambiguous conditions. My work spans industry R&D, academic research, and technical education — grounded in a Ph.D. in Biomedical Engineering from Dartmouth College and postdoctoral training at Massachusetts General Hospital and Harvard Medical School.

experience.title: Sr. Staff Scientist - R&D PD, Home
bullets: Lead cross-functional R&D programs in peritoneal dialysis, driving end-to-end development of therapy concepts from early-stage research through translational and product development
Lead microbiology laboratory activities, including contamination assessment and risk evaluation, contributing directly to improved product safety, reliability, and patient outcomes
Manage and mentor laboratory personnel and scientists, including technical oversight, task prioritization, interviewing, hiring support, and performance development activities
Drive scientific and technical strategy within the Therapy Concept and Research group, helping define, develop, and execute Value Stream PD strategies aligned with organizational priorities
Provide technical leadership in advanced modeling, simulation, and data-driven analysis to support therapy design, optimization, and performance evaluation
Oversee multiple concurrent R&D initiatives across engineering, clinical, and research functions, ensuring execution against program milestones and strategic objectives
Contribute to intellectual property development, including invention disclosure, technical documentation, and support for patentable innovations in PD technologies
Lead cross-functional collaboration across clinical, engineering, regulatory, and manufacturing stakeholders, ensuring translational alignment from concept to product development
Lead and mentor scientists, engineers, and laboratory staff, strengthening technical capability, execution, and cross-functional collaboration across the organization

experience.title: Staff Scientist - R&D PD, Home
bullets: Led advanced modeling and simulation initiatives supporting peritoneal dialysis therapy development and system optimization
Contributed to strategic R&D programs focused on improving clinical performance and patient outcomes
Designed and implemented computational frameworks for data-driven analysis of therapy performance
Supported cross-functional collaboration between engineering, clinical, and product teams
Played a key role in internal research initiatives and technology evaluation efforts
Recognized for contributions to scientific innovation and applied research excellence

experience.title: Training & Instruction Manager, College Pathways
bullets: Led curriculum development and program delivery across multiple university sites, ensuring consistency, quality, and scalability.

Managed and led a distributed team of instructors and technical staff supporting training programs in data science, ML, and responsible AI, including hiring, onboarding, performance management, and curriculum oversight.
Designed and implemented training frameworks to support instructors and improve program effectiveness.
Drove program improvements through data-driven evaluation of outcomes and participant feedback.
Directed cross-functional program delivery and coordinated instructors, technical contributors, and external partners across multiple concurrent initiatives.

experience.title: Mathematics Teacher
bullets: Designed and delivered mathematics curriculum in an international academic setting.
Fostered student engagement and critical thinking through rigorous, student-centered instruction.
Collaborated with faculty across disciplines to support a holistic educational experience.
Adapted teaching methods to diverse cultural and academic backgrounds.

experience.title: Instructor in Mathematics
bullets: Delivered advanced mathematics instruction within a highly rigorous academic environment.
Emphasized problem-solving, analytical thinking, and student-led learning methodologies.
Mentored students academically and supported their intellectual development.

experience.title: Director, Center for Quantitative Obesity Research
bullets: Directed strategy, operations, and research portfolio for an interdisciplinary center focused on quantitative modeling and data-driven approaches to obesity and metabolic health.
Led development and execution of multi-investigator research initiatives spanning mathematics, biomedical science, and clinical collaboration.
Secured and supported research funding efforts, including institutional grants, to sustain and expand center activities.
Built and managed cross-functional academic and clinical collaborations, integrating expertise across engineering, medicine, and data science disciplines.
Oversaw research program development, ensuring alignment with institutional priorities, funding opportunities, and scholarly impact objectives.
Provided organizational leadership for research dissemination, including publications, presentations, and external academic engagement initiatives.

experience.title: Assistant Professor of Mathematics
bullets: Held tenure-track faculty appointment with responsibilities spanning teaching, research, and academic leadership across undergraduate and graduate applied mathematics programs.
Taught a broad range of courses from introductory mathematics to advanced graduate-level applied mathematics, serving a diverse student population.
Received the Excellence in Teaching Award (2018) from The National Society of Leadership and Success in recognition of sustained instructional excellence.

Secured competitive research funding, including NSF support, Separately Budgeted Research Awards, Nussbaum Research Grant, and Faculty-Student Research Awards supporting an active independent research program.
Coordinated key academic programs, including PreCalculus and Calculus/BioCalculus sequences, contributing to curriculum design, consistency, and student success outcomes.
Supervised and mentored undergraduate and graduate students, and a postdoctoral researcher, while also coordinating key academic programs and serving in departmental leadership and liaison roles.

experience.company: Massachusetts General Hospital / Harvard Medical School
experience.title: Postdoctoral Research Fellow
bullets: Conducted postdoctoral research in the Pulmonary Imaging and Bioengineering Laboratory under Dr. Tilo Winkler.
Investigated airway behavior during asthma attacks using a comprehensive mathematical lung model and extensive computational simulations validated by in-vivo animal studies.
Developed computational models and imaging methodologies for quantitative analysis of physiological systems.
Co-chaired the MGH Postdoc Association, growing membership and programming significantly during tenure.
Collaborated with interdisciplinary teams of clinicians, engineers, and researchers on NIH-supported projects, publishing peer-reviewed research and presenting at national scientific conferences.

certifications.title: Project Planning: Putting It All Together
Project Initiation: Starting a Successful Project

projects.title: PD Kinetic Model Simulator
projects.description: Developed an iterative computational simulator to model peritoneal dialysis kinetics and support therapy optimization.
projects.impact: Advanced through 14+ progressive iterations and informed therapy design decisions across Fresenius PD programs.

projects.title: Sensor Testing & PD Connectology Study
projects.description: Led a program of sensor testing experiments comparing FME vs. Baxter CAPD and developing in vitro test setups for evidence generation.
projects.impact: Completed 175+ sensor-testing experiments and produced results that shaped strategic PD connectology decisions.

projects.title: Quantitative Obesity Research Center
projects.description: Established and led an interdisciplinary academic research center focused on quantitative modeling of metabolic health and obesity.
projects.impact: Secured NSF Co-PI funding including a $300K award and built cross-disciplinary clinical research collaborations.

projects.title: Adjunct Mathematics Instructor Resources & Support
projects.description: Led an NSF-funded initiative to develop resources and support for adjunct mathematics instructors, strengthening instructional quality and professional development.
projects.impact: Helped improve adjunct teaching outcomes and instructor retention through evidence-based academic support, mentorship, and instructional resources.

recommendations.name: Eduardo Salcedo
recommendations.title: Assistant Professor, Miami Dade College
recommendations.text:
I had the privilege of working directly under Amir's leadership for nearly two years, and I can say without hesitation: working under Amir was the best professional experience of my career.
Amir, above all, is a leader who invests deeply in the people around him. He didn't merely manage tasks; he genuinely cared about my professional growth. He assigned me challenging projects that pushed me beyond my comfort zone, provided constructive and thoughtful feedback, and consistently created opportunities for me to develop new skills. He led by example, setting a standard of excellence while empowering the team to make meaningful decisions independently. Micromanagement was simply not in his vocabulary.
As a communicator, Amir was exceptional. He ensured that the team remained aligned and informed, removing any surprises or ambiguity regarding direction or expectations. He consistently made himself accessible and approachable, actively fostering an environment where open dialogue was encouraged, even when dissenting views were expressed. This genuine sense of being heard made a significant positive impact on the team.
Amir's most remarkable trait was his unwavering integrity. When things didn't go as planned, he took full responsibility rather than deflecting blame onto his team. He actively advocated for us to senior leadership, fought for our recognition, and consistently treated everyone with fairness and impartiality. People always knew where they stood with him, and they deeply respected him for it.
The culture Amir cultivated was truly exceptional. He created a psychologically safe environment where individuals felt empowered to take risks, share their ideas, and deliver their best work. Our team's morale was high not because things were always easy, but because people genuinely wanted to perform for him. Amir's ability to retain strong talent and attract others who shared his vision for what he was building made his legacy truly remarkable.
Any organization would be fortunate to have Amir in a leadership role. I give him my highest recommendation.

recommendations.name: Roshni Kasad
recommendations.title: Program and Partnerships Management | Passionate about Education and Equity
recommendations.text: Amir and I worked together while at AI4ALL on the College Pathways initiative. He has a unique combo of technical, pedagogical, and project management expertise. When approached with a challenge, Amir is calm and collected about how to resolve it. He is incredibly thoughtful in his approach and thoroughly dependable in executing his work. I would be excited for the opportunity to work with him again at some point. In addition to being incredibly effective at his work, he also adds a sense of warmth and fun to the team culture.

recommendations.name: Bogdan G. Nita
recommendations.title: Professor at Montclair State University
recommendations.text: I have known Amir for more than 10 years and we have been colleagues in the Department of Mathematics at Montclair State University for more than 5 years. Amir is an outstanding researcher, teacher and mentor, a great colleague ready to take on any service task no matter how small or difficult, and a great human being in general. He is loved by students who consider him one of the best professors in our Department. He is a pleasure to interact with and a valuable asset to any College.

recommendations.name: Ann Skoczenski, PhD
recommendations.title: Director of Postdoctoral Services, Massachusetts Institute of Technology
recommendations.text: As a postdoctoral research fellow at Massachusetts General Hospital, Amir joined the Mass General Postdoc Association (MGPA), a group that I advise, which is organized by and for MGH research fellows. Amir quickly demonstrated his organizational and leadership skills, and within a year he took on the position of co-chair, the top executive position in the association. Thanks to Amir's skills and dedication, the MGPA membership and programming grew significantly during his tenure as co-chair.

recommendations.name: Marina Savransky, MA, CRA
recommendations.title: Supervisor, Grants & Agreements at Hackensack Meridian Health, Center for Discovery & Innovation (CDI)
recommendations.text: I assisted Amir on several of his grant applications to various Federal and private foundations. Amir is very passionate about his research and always sought to create very strong proposals to advance his research. His hard work paid off as he was awarded prestigious grants supporting his work from agencies such as the National Science Foundation (NSF). Amir is both a strong individual researcher as well as an excellent collaborator in teams. It was always a pleasure to work with Amir, I highly endorse his research and grantsmanship skills.
